Give me a short answer about why the sky appears blue.","type":"text"}],"role":"user"},{"content":[{"text":"The sky appears blue due to **Rayleigh scattering**. Sunlight contains all colors, but when it hits Earth's atmosphere, shorter blue wavelengths scatter more than longer red wavelengths. This scattered blue light reaches our eyes from all directions, making the sky look blue.","type":"text"}],"role":"assistant"},{"content":[{"text":"Now answer the same question again in exactly one sentence.","type":"text"}],"role":"user"}],"model":"claude-opus-4-5","stream":true,"system":"You are a concise assistant.
